failure_modes.text: Maslow's hammer can force a familiar frame onto a system that needs a different cut.
failure_modes.source_quote: Incorrectly applying a familiar framework to a novel problem (I have seen this one before, it is an X problem).
failure_modes.mitigation: Test frame fit before applying: verify that the systems lens captures the dominant dynamics rather than forcing a familiar framework onto a problem that needs a different cut.

failure_modes.text: Confirmation bias and groupthink can lock the team into the current hypothesis or authority vision.
failure_modes.source_quote: The tendency to seek or interpret information that confirms existing hypotheses or the vision of authority figures.
failure_modes.mitigation: Introduce adversarial perspectives: rotate in an outsider or devil's advocate to challenge the dominant hypothesis before consensus hardens.

failure_modes.text: Architecture can be used as a misdiagnosis when the real problem is coordination, ownership, or feedback design.
failure_modes.source_quote: Architecture debt is used as the explanation when the recurring failures are actually coordination debt or ownership ambiguity.
failure_modes.mitigation: Separate architecture problems from coordination problems: when the system diagram looks right but outcomes are wrong, check whether the real issue is ownership, incentives, or feedback loops.

failure_modes.text: Analysis paralysis can keep the team in endless system explanation without moving toward the next intervention.
failure_modes.source_quote: Getting lost in the complexity and depth of the system, becoming unable to move from thorough investigation to action.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set an analysis deadline: define when systems explanation must produce a concrete intervention, and enforce the boundary to prevent mapping from becoming an end in itself.

premortem_questions.text: What would you have to believe?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Use tools like What would you have to believe? to spell out all implicit assumptions and implications of a viewpoint.
premortem_questions.note: The question is explicit; the source quote comes from the mitigation table.

premortem_questions.text: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: For large projects, ask, Is the worst case bad enough? to ensure sufficient weight is placed on low-probability, high-impact outcomes.
premortem_questions.note: Preserves the raw premortem question exactly, with surrounding source support.

heuristics.text: Expose mental models to the light of day so assumptions become visible, checkable, and revisable.
heuristics.source_quote: A key practice is to expose mental models to the light of day.

heuristics.text: Run the discussion at two levels: the case at hand and the machine that produced it.
heuristics.source_quote: discussions should be conducted at two levels: the machine level ... and the case-at-hand level

heuristics.text: Model the system dynamically with causal loops or stock-and-flow views before choosing the fix.
heuristics.source_quote: Systems thinkers utilize tools like causal loop diagrams and stock and flow diagrams to visualize the system.

heuristics.text: Design strategic metrics by starting from the goal, then mapping the process, measures, and levers that can move it.
heuristics.source_quote: This involves first knowing the goal, then understanding the process ... identifying the key points in the process that are best for measurement, and finally, exploring how to create levers.
heuristics.note: Condenses the raw strategic metric design sequence into a chunk-ready heuristic.

curation_notes.summary: The raw file is strong on leverage-seeking heuristics and architecture-misdiagnosis failure content, even though its premortem section is sparse.
donor_drops: Deferred donor compound-contract ideas because this wave is still first-order chunk semantics only.
open_questions: A later wave may want a dedicated leverage-point subfield, but this run keeps the contract narrow.

allies: Raw allies section is strong on multidisciplinary mental models, problem-solving methodologies, System 2, and analogy.
antagonists: Raw antagonists section provides future richness on law of the instrument, specialization, and WYSIATI.
structured_tensions: This source file has no explicit structured tension block, but its raw antagonist material is rich enough for later relation work.
